label: Uber
description: Connect Uber to access ride history, Eats deliveries, and Business account data via the Uber API.

form.title: Connect Uber
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read your Uber rides, Eats, and Business data via OAuth 2.0.

methods.label: Sign in with Uber (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: OAuth 2.0 — opens Uber's consent screen in your browser. Requires creating a free Uber developer app at developer.uber.com to get a Client ID and Client Secret.
methods.how_to:
## Sign in with Uber (OAuth 2.0)

You'll need a free Uber developer app to get OAuth credentials. This takes about 5 minutes.

### Create an Uber developer app

1. Go to [developer.uber.com](https://developer.uber.com) and sign in with your Uber account.
2. Click **My Apps → Create App**.
3. Give the app a name like `Anton` and fill in any required description fields.
4. Under **Redirect URIs**, add `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect for the OAuth callback).
5. Under **Scopes**, enable the ones you need:
   - `profile` — your name and email
   - `history` — past trip history
   - `rides.request` / `rides.read` — request and read ride data
   - `eats.deliveries` — Uber Eats delivery data
6. Save the app. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app dashboard.

### Connect in Anton

7. Paste the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into the fields below.
8. Choose which **scopes** you want Anton to request (leave the default for full access).
9. Click **Submit**. A browser window opens to Uber's consent screen.
10. Sign in and click **Allow**. Anton stores the resulting tokens locally.

### Notes

- `rides.request` lets Anton request rides on your behalf — remove it if you only need read access.
- Uber tokens expire; Anton will refresh them automatically using the refresh token.
- To revoke access, visit [privacy.uber.com](https://privacy.uber.com) → **Account → Apps with Access** and remove Anton.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: Found on your app's dashboard at developer.uber.com under App Credentials.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Keep this private — anyone with it can impersonate your app.

fields.name: scopes
fields.label: Scopes
fields.description: Choose the permission set Anton should request. Match these to the scopes enabled in your Uber app.
